content.problemStatement:
Chef Leonardo has a decimal integer NNN and a non-zero decimal digit ddd. NNN does not contain the digit zero; specifically, NNN should always be treated as a decimal integer without leading zeroes.

Chef likes ddd and does not like any other digit, so he decided to change NNN. He may apply the following operation any number of times (including zero): append the digit ddd to the decimal representation of NNN (ddd becomes the least significant digit of NNN), then remove one occurrence of one digit from the decimal representation of NNN.

Chef has already changed a lot of numbers and he got bored. Now, he wants to know the smallest possible value of NNN which can be obtained by performing the operation described above. Can you help him?
constraints: NNN does not contain the digit 000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and ddd.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the minimum number Chef can obtain.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Chef can remove the digit 555 and add 444 to the end of NNN. Then, NNN becomes 343434.
Example case 2: Chef can remove the digit 444 from the beginning of NNN, add 444 to the end of NNN and get N=24N = 24N=24.
Example case 3: Chef cannot make his number any smaller.
